title: Rocket Lab launches 10th Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its 10th radar-imaging satellite for Japanese company Synspective on June 26, a mission that was delayed due to a responsive space operation.

title: Spain-backed fund joins FOSSA’s sovereign satellite communications push
summary: Spanish startup FOSSA Systems raised $10.5 million for its sovereign satellite communications constellation, with a Spain-backed fund participating in the round.

title: Starlink veterans launch startup to broaden megaconstellation ownership
summary: Former SpaceX engineers who contributed to Starlink have founded a new startup to expand megaconstellation ownership for governments and companies. The initiative aims to provide greater control over space-based infrastructure.

title: ISS repair spacewalk highlights concerns about station health
summary: NASA astronauts are preparing for a spacewalk to repair a robotic arm on the ISS, while advisers express concerns about the station's long-term health and spacesuit reliability.

title: Europe’s next security challenge is in orbit
summary: Europe faces a security challenge in space as satellite navigation underpins over 10% of its GDP. The reliance on space-based systems highlights the critical importance of orbital security.

title: Rocket Lab wins NASA award for three Electron launches
summary: NASA selected Rocket Lab to launch two science missions using three Electron rockets in 2027. This award highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting NASA's scientific exploration efforts.

title: Weather Stream releases first light imagery from GEMS2 microwave radiometer
summary: Weather Stream has released the first light imagery from its GEMS2 microwave radiometer aboard the GEMS2-Amethyst satellite. The company, based in Boulder, Colorado, is now collecting global atmospheric observations.

title: Space Force’s new MUOS satellites to use Boeing’s 702MP spacecraft platform
summary: The U.S. Space Force will deploy new MUOS satellites using Boeing's 702MP spacecraft platform, which has previously supported commercial and military wideband missions.

title: Botswana signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Botswana has signed the Artemis Accords, joining other nations in adopting best practices for space exploration. This move aligns the country with international efforts to promote sustainable and collaborative space activities.

title: Small satellite operators confront a bottleneck to space access
summary: Small satellite operators face challenges accessing space due to reliance on SpaceX's rideshare missions. The bottleneck highlights the need for alternative launch solutions.

title: Black hole's 'point of no escape' studied with the loudest gravitational waves ever heard
summary: Scientists detected the loudest gravitational waves ever recorded, which may help study black hole event horizons. This discovery provides new insights into the boundaries where nothing can escape black holes.

title: SpaceX's next Starship breathes fire for 1st time in prelaunch test (video)
summary: SpaceX conducted a prelaunch test firing of its next Starship rocket, marking progress in preparations for its next launch. This follows the first launch of the 'Version 3' Starship just a month prior.

title: China plans to double the size of its Tiangong space station while the ISS nears its end
summary: China intends to expand its Tiangong space station by adding three new modules and a co-orbiting space telescope. This move comes as the International Space Station (ISS) approaches its planned decommissioning and deorbit.

title: Only a few hours left! This expert-approved, beginner-friendly drone is $60 off for Prime Day
summary: A beginner-friendly drone, the DJI Neo, is being offered at a $60 discount during Prime Day, making it $139. Drone expert James Abbott recommends it for newcomers.

title: Moore's law tells us that the price of technology should go down over time, so why is stargazing gear getting more expensive?
summary: The prices of stargazing gear, including smart telescopes and image-stabilized binoculars, are rising despite Moore's Law suggesting technology should become cheaper over time. Factors like demand, supply chain issues, and advanced features are driving up costs across the market.

title: Beam me up to 4K: Your ultimate sci-fi collection for less than $25 on Prime Day
summary: Amazon is offering the Fire TV Stick 4K Plus at a significant discount during Prime Day, making it an affordable option for streaming. This deal is available for a limited time today.

title: Watch out for the giant scorpion hiding in the summer sky
summary: The news highlights the constellation Scorpius, which resembles its namesake and is visible in the summer sky. It encourages stargazers to observe it during this season.

title: Watch a 'Potentially hazardous asteroid' the size of a skyscraper close in on Earth live online tonight
summary: A potentially hazardous asteroid the size of a skyscraper will make a close approach to Earth tonight, an event that occurs roughly once every decade.

title: Neon green auroras from space will take your breath away | Space photo of the day for June 26, 2026
summary: A stunning space photo captures neon green auroras, offering a breathtaking view unlike any other. The image is featured as Space photo of the day for June 26, 2026.

title: NASA Announces Winners for 2026 Human Lander Challenge
summary: NASA revealed the winners of the 2026 Human Lander Challenge, honoring student-developed solutions for lunar lander life support systems. The competition supports the Artemis program, aiming to return astronauts to the Moon.

title: NASA Tests New Refuel Device for Future In-Space Refueling Missions
summary: NASA is testing a new refueling device for future in-space missions to enable spacecraft to refuel in Earth orbit before deep space exploration. The technology resembles a gas pump nozzle but is designed for spacecraft fuel tanks.

title: Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission
summary: NASA and partners are preparing for the June 30 launch of the Swift Boost Mission to raise the orbit of NASA’s Swift observatory. This mission aims to extend the operational lifespan of the observatory.

title: NASA Identifies More Than 40 Space Technologies for Collaboration
summary: NASA selected 41 proposals from 37 companies to advance space technologies supporting lunar and Martian exploration. These collaborations focus on space transportation, planetary surface operations, and lunar infrastructure.

title: Euclid Sees Heart of Milky Way
summary: Euclid, an ESA mission with NASA contributions, captured a new image of the heart of the Milky Way galaxy. The observation aligns with a region that NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will study after its upcoming launch.

title: Bringing Signals to NASA
summary: Eric Fernandez's childhood fascination with rocket launches led him to a career in the space industry, inspired by his grandparents' service at Vandenberg Air Force Base.

title: NASA’s PACE Mission Studies Smoke, Fires
summary: NASA's PACE satellite is monitoring vegetation precursors to fires, smoke plumes, and their movement during the North American fire season. The data will help scientists better understand wildfires.

title: Hubble Spies Starry Chandelier
summary: The NASA/ESA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the globular cluster NGC 6723, also known as the Chandelier Cluster. This cluster is a dense collection of stars bound together by gravity.

title: The Battle for Sullivan’s Island
summary: The Battle for Sullivan’s Island involved colonial forces repelling British troops on June 28, 1776, using the island's challenging terrain near Charleston, South Carolina.

title: Expedition 73 Crew Reflects on Science, Teamwork, and Life in Orbit
summary: Astronauts and cosmonauts from Expedition 73 gathered at Space Center Houston to share mission experiences and honor ground teams. The event highlighted teamwork and contributions from NASA's SpaceX Crew-10, Soyuz MS-27, and NASA's SpaceX Crew-11 missions.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new modifications.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the global launch manifest.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. The Canadarm2 is a critical robotic arm used for maintenance and operations on the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing toward the 2026 debut of its Terran R heavy-lift vehicle after transporting the second stage to Stennis Space Center. The company is making significant progress in developing the rocket.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. These milestones follow the recent Flight 12 mission.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A breakthrough in robotic arm technology is highlighted as a significant advancement for the space industry. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and on-orbit operations.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The programme facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is leading this effort with innovative contributions from early-career scientists.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. The accompanying image is not described, leaving the specific details unclear.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: Former SpaceX employees have collectively raised $9.2 billion for their new ventures, highlighting SpaceX's role in fostering engineering talent and innovation. The company's 24-year history has seen thousands of engineers transition to other projects.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered vision navigation and autonomous guidance systems provider. This acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's autonomous space operations capabilities.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to meet growing demand. This expansion aims to foster competition and innovation in the space industry.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission to address hacking threats and shared-compute modules in rideshare satellites. Their collaboration focuses on Space Fabric, a solution designed to enhance on-orbit security.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan plans to select a proposal this month for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO. The initiative aims to enhance Japan's sovereign satellite capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Canada’s Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association, a new trade group for NGSO (Non-Geostationary Orbit) satellite operators.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a significant step in the reentry sector. The event highlights growing competition and innovation in space reentry technologies.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches. A report from the space agency's inspector general highlights this impending challenge.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space has raised $7 million and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit. The initiatives aim to advance the company's orbital-compute capabilities.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted the readiness of commercial markets for Earth Observation (EO) solutions but noted a persistent gap in understanding how to effectively communicate these solutions to customers.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Galaxy Broadband Communications for up to $52 million CAD ($36.6 million USD) to expand its satellite communications and remote connectivity solutions. The deal highlights Calian's strategic growth in the Canadian space industry.

title: Tototheo Global to Deploy SES FlexMaritime in Expanded Maritime Deal
summary: Tototheo Global has expanded its agreement with SES to deploy the FlexMaritime connectivity service for the maritime market. The service combines SES's satellite network with integrated solutions.

title: ReOrbit Secures Finnish Funding for ‘OrbitCloud’ R&D Program
summary: ReOrbit has secured €4.6 million ($5.3 million) in Finnish funding from Business Finland for its new OrbitCloud R&D program aimed at developing a space-based edge computing network.

title: AiRanaculus to Advance Networking Technologies for NASA Lunar and Space Communications Networks
summary: AiRanaculus received a $5 million award from NASA to develop networking technologies for lunar and space communications networks. The technology will support NASA's future lunar and deep-space missions.

title: Infleqtion Teams With Voyager in New Quantum Space Initiative
summary: Infleqtion and Voyager Technologies are collaborating with other entities on a new quantum space initiative to advance U.S. quantum space ambitions. The partnership includes Monarch Quantum, Armada, and the University of Colorado Boulder.

title: Pegasus Launch Set to Deploy Servicer Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus rocket is scheduled to launch a servicing spacecraft to reboost NASA's Swift Observatory before it re-enters Earth's atmosphere. This mission aims to extend the observatory's operational life.

title: Mitsubishi Electric Contracts MDA Space for Digital Payload for Japanese Defense Satellite
summary: Mitsubishi Electric Corporation has contracted MDA Space to develop a digital payload for Japan's next-generation defense communications satellite. The contract includes the construction of digital payloads and antennas.

title: Capella Validates Mynaric Optical Terminal on Latest SAR Satellite
summary: Capella Space validated a Mynaric optical communications terminal on its latest SAR satellite, marking the first deployment of an optical terminal by Capella. The company also released the first synthetic aperture radar images from the satellite.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct satellite buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two expected to become operational by 2029.
